devanagari: अथैकविंशोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । सत्यं समीक्ष्याब्जभवो नखेन्दुभिर्हतस्वधामद्युतिरावृतोऽभ्यगात् । मरीचिमिश्रा ऋषयो बृहद्व्रताः सनन्दनाद्...
english_devnagari: athaikaviṃśo'dhyāyaḥśrīśuka uvācasatyaṃ samīkṣyābjabhavo nakhendubhirhatasvadhāmadyutirāvṛto'bhyagātmarīcimiśrā ṛṣayo bṛhadvratāḥ sanandanādyā naradeva yoginaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
On beholding that the foot of the Lord had reached (his region) satya-loka and finding himself enveloped in and the effulgence of his region eclipsed by the brilliant lustre of his moon-like (toe-) nails, lotus-born god Brahmā and also great sages like Marīci, sages of great vows (of life-long celebacy like Nārada) and yogins (of the path of knowledge) like Sanandana, came forth to receive him.

devanagari: वेदोपवेदा नियमा यमान्वितास्तर्केतिहासाङ्गपुराणसंहिताः । ये चापरे योगसमीरदीपित ज्ञानाग्निना रन्धितकर्मकल्मषाः । ववन्दिरे यत्स्मरणानुभावतः...
english_devnagari: vedopavedā niyamā yamānvitāstarketihāsāṅgapurāṇasaṃhitāḥye cāpare yogasamīradīpita jñānāgninā randhitakarmakalmaṣāḥvavandire yatsmaraṇānubhāvataḥ svāyambhuvaṃ dhāma gatā akarmakam
translation: (The presiding deities of) the Vedas and the subsidiary Vedas (viz. Āyur-veda the science of medicine, Dhanur-veda the military science, Gāndharva-veda the science of music. Sthāpatya-veda the science of Engineering), Niyamas and yamas[1], science of Logic (Sāṃkhya and Mīmāṃsā), History (viz. the Rāmāyaṇa and the Mahābhārata), six auxiliaries of Vedas,[2] Purāṇas and Saṃhitās (sacred texts like Pāñcarātra Saṃhitā, Brahma-saṃhitā, Garga-saṃhitā and others) who had burnt down their impurities of sins by the fire of spiritual knowledge enkindled by the wind of Yoga. approached and bowed down at the foot of the Lord, by virtue of meditating which they would attain to the region of god Brahmā which is not accessible by the path of Karmas (religious, ritualistic acts).
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Vide Supra Bhāgavata Purāṇa 4.22.24.

Footnote [2]:
viz. (1) Śikṣā—phonetics or Science of proper articulation and pronunciation (2) Chandas— science of Prosody, (3) Vyākaraṇa—Grammar, (4) Nirukta— Etymology of Vedic words, (5) Jyotiṣa—Astronomy, (6) Kalpa—code of rituals.

devanagari: अथाङ्घ्रये प्रोन्नमिताय विष्णोरुपाहरत्पद्मभवोऽर्हणोदकम् । समर्च्य भक्त्याभ्यगृणाच्छुचिश्रवा यन्नाभिपङ्केरुहसम्भवः स्वयम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: athāṅghraye pronnamitāya viṣṇorupāharatpadmabhavo'rhaṇodakamsamarcya bhaktyābhyagṛṇācchuciśravā yannābhipaṅkeruhasambhavaḥ svayam
translation: Thereupon, the lotus-born god Brahmā, of extremely pure reputation, brought water to worship Viṣṇu’s foot raised upto his region. Devoutly worshipping his feet, he sang out his praise, for he himself was born out of the lotus that adorns his navel.

devanagari: धातुः कमण्डलुजलं तदुरुक्रमस्य पादावनेजनपवित्रतया नरेन्द्र । स्वर्धुन्यभून्नभसि सा पतती निमार्ष्टि लोकत्रयं भगवतो विशदेव कीर्तिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: dhātuḥ kamaṇḍalujalaṃ tadurukramasya pādāvanejanapavitratayā narendrasvardhunyabhūnnabhasi sā patatī nimārṣṭi lokatrayaṃ bhagavato viśadeva kīrtiḥ
translation: The water that was poured out of the Kamaṇḍalu (water-jug) of god Brahmā, for washing the feet of the Lord of mighty strides, and being purified thereby, became the heavenly river in the sky. Like the pure hallowing renown of the Lord, it sanctifies the three worlds as it falls from the sky, and flows through them from the heavens.

devanagari: ब्रह्मादयो लोकनाथाः स्वनाथाय समादृताः । सानुगा बलिमाजह्रुः सङ्क्षिप्तात्मविभूतये ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmādayo lokanāthāḥ svanāthāya samādṛtāḥsānugā balimājahruḥ saṅkṣiptātmavibhūtaye
translation: God Brahmā and other protectors of the worlds, along with their followers, reverentially brought articles of worship to their Lord who had withdrawn his all-pervading form in a limited personality (viz. that of Vāmana).

devanagari: तोयैः समर्हणैः स्रग्भिर्दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनैः । धूपैर्दीपैः सुरभिभिर्लाजाक्षतफलाङ्कुरैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: toyaiḥ samarhaṇaiḥ sragbhirdivyagandhānulepanaiḥdhūpairdīpaiḥ surabhibhirlājākṣataphalāṅkuraiḥ
translation: With water brought for worship, with garlands of flowers and divinely fragrant sandal-paste and pigment, with burning of fragrant incense and lights, parched grains of paddy, unbroken rice, fruits and sprouts.

devanagari: स्तवनैर्जयशब्दैश्च तद्वीर्यमहिमाङ्कितैः । नृत्यवादित्रगीतैश्च शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिःस्वनैः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: stavanairjayaśabdaiśca tadvīryamahimāṅkitaiḥnṛtyavāditragītaiśca śaṅkhadundubhiniḥsvanaiḥ
translation: With songs of praise, shouts of victory announcing his prowess and greatness, with dancing performances, playing of musical instruments and presentation of vocal music and sounds of conchs and kettle drums.

devanagari: जाम्बवानृक्षराजस्तु भेरीशब्दैर्मनोजवः । विजयं दिक्षु सर्वासु महोत्सवमघोषयत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: jāmbavānṛkṣarājastu bherīśabdairmanojavaḥvijayaṃ dikṣu sarvāsu mahotsavamaghoṣayat
translation: Jāmbavān, the king of bears, with the speed of the mind, proclaimed with beating of drums in all directions, the victory of the Lord and the great festive occasion.

devanagari: महीं सर्वां हृतां दृष्ट्वा त्रिपदव्याजयाञ्चया । ऊचुः स्वभर्तुरसुरा दीक्षितस्यात्यमर्षिताः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: mahīṃ sarvāṃ hṛtāṃ dṛṣṭvā tripadavyājayāñcayāūcuḥ svabharturasurā dīkṣitasyātyamarṣitāḥ
translation: Finding that under a deceitful pretext of a request of three feet of land, the entire earth was taken away (fraudulently) from their master who was engaged in a sacrifice, the Asuras flared up and said to each other.

devanagari: न वायं ब्रह्मबन्धुर्विष्णुर्मायाविनां वरः । द्विजरूपप्रतिच्छन्नो देवकार्यं चिकीर्षति ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: na vāyaṃ brahmabandhurviṣṇurmāyāvināṃ varaḥdvijarūpapraticchanno devakāryaṃ cikīrṣati
translation: “Certainly this is not even a Brāhmaṇa by mere caste, (even a lowly Brāhmaṇa will not commit such a fraud). He is Viṣṇu, the prominent one among the masters of illusive powers (and conjuring tricks). Disguising himself as a Brāhmaṇa, he desires to accomplish the objective of gods.

devanagari: अनेन याचमानेन शत्रुणा वटुरूपिणा । सर्वस्वं नो हृतं भर्तुर्न्यस्तदण्डस्य बर्हिषि ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: anena yācamānena śatruṇā vaṭurūpiṇāsarvasvaṃ no hṛtaṃ bharturnyastadaṇḍasya barhiṣi
translation: We stand cheated of everything by this enemy who assuming the disguise of a Brahmacārin came for begging, while our master has laid down his powers of punishment during the period of this sacrifice.

devanagari: सत्यव्रतस्य सततं दीक्षितस्य विशेषतः । नानृतं भाषितुं शक्यं ब्रह्मण्यस्य दयावतः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: satyavratasya satataṃ dīkṣitasya viśeṣataḥnānṛtaṃ bhāṣituṃ śakyaṃ brahmaṇyasya dayāvataḥ
translation: Being a person vowed to observe (perpetual) truthfulness, and especially when consecrated for performing sacrifice, it is not possible to utter falsehood for our master who is friendly to Brāhmaṇas full of compassion.

devanagari: तस्मादस्य वधो धर्मो भर्तुः शुश्रूषणं च नः । इत्यायुधानि जगृहुर्बलेरनुचरासुराः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādasya vadho dharmo bhartuḥ śuśrūṣaṇaṃ ca naḥityāyudhāni jagṛhurbaleranucarāsurāḥ
translation: It is, therefore, our sacred duty to kill him (Vāmana) and serve the cause of our master.” Saying thus the followers of Bali, Asuras, took up their arms.

devanagari: ते सर्वे वामनं हन्तुं शूलपट्टिशपाणयः । अनिच्छन्तो बले राजन्प्राद्रवन्जातमन्यवः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: te sarve vāmanaṃ hantuṃ śūlapaṭṭiśapāṇayaḥanicchanto bale rājanprādravanjātamanyavaḥ
translation: Thus being enraged, all of them, armed with spears (śūla) and sharp-edged steel-bars[3] (paṭṭiśa) rushed to strike at Vāmana (the Dwarf), even though Bali did not wish it.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
paṭṭiśo lohadaṇḍo yas tīkṣṇadhāraḥ kṣuropamaḥ—Vaijayantī quoted in ASD. p. 310.

devanagari: तानभिद्रवतो दृष्ट्वा दितिजानीकपान्नृप । प्रहस्यानुचरा विष्णोः प्रत्यषेधन्नुदायुधाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: tānabhidravato dṛṣṭvā ditijānīkapānnṛpaprahasyānucarā viṣṇoḥ pratyaṣedhannudāyudhāḥ
translation: Seeing the generals of the Daitya army attacking Vāmana, Viṣṇu’s attendants laughed at them, and thwarted them (Asuras’ attack) with their arms uplifted.

devanagari: नन्दः सुनन्दोऽथ जयो विजयः प्रबलो बलः । कुमुदः कुमुदाक्षश्च विष्वक्सेनः पतत्त्रिराट् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: nandaḥ sunando'tha jayo vijayaḥ prabalo balaḥkumudaḥ kumudākṣaśca viṣvaksenaḥ patattrirāṭ
translation: Nanda, Sunanda, Jaya, Vijaya, Prabala, Bala, Kumuda, Kumudākṣa, Viśvaksena, the king of birds—Garuḍa, Śrutadeva, Puṣpadanta and Sātvata—each of them possessing the strength of ten thousand elephants, advanced to kill the Asura army.

devanagari: जयन्तः श्रुतदेवश्च पुष्पदन्तोऽथ सात्वतः । सर्वे नागायुतप्राणाश्चमूं ते जघ्नुरासुरीम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: jayantaḥ śrutadevaśca puṣpadanto'tha sātvataḥsarve nāgāyutaprāṇāścamūṃ te jaghnurāsurīm
translation: Nanda, Sunanda, Jaya, Vijaya, Prabala, Bala, Kumuda, Kumudākṣa, Viśvaksena, the king of birds—Garuḍa, Śrutadeva, Puṣpadanta and Sātvata—each of them possessing the strength of ten thousand elephants, advanced to kill the Asura army.

devanagari: हन्यमानान्स्वकान्दृष्ट्वा पुरुषानुचरैर्बलिः । वारयामास संरब्धान्काव्यशापमनुस्मरन् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: hanyamānānsvakāndṛṣṭvā puruṣānucarairbaliḥvārayāmāsa saṃrabdhānkāvyaśāpamanusmaran
translation: Seeing his people being massacred by the followers of Viṣṇu, and remembering the curse of Śukra (his preceptor), Bali prevented them from fighting, although they were enraged.

devanagari: हे विप्रचित्ते हे राहो हे नेमे श्रूयतां वचः । मा युध्यत निवर्तध्वं न नः कालोऽयमर्थकृत् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: he vipracitte he rāho he neme śrūyatāṃ vacaḥmā yudhyata nivartadhvaṃ na naḥ kālo'yamarthakṛt
translation: “Oh Vipracitti! Oh Rāhu! Oh Nemi! Listen to my speech! Please do not fight; retreat. This period is not favourable unto us.

devanagari: यः प्रभुः सर्वभूतानां सुखदुःखोपपत्तये । तं नातिवर्तितुं दैत्याः पौरुषैरीश्वरः पुमान् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ prabhuḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ sukhaduḥkhopapattayetaṃ nātivartituṃ daityāḥ pauruṣairīśvaraḥ pumān
translation: Oh Daitya comrades! No human being is powerful enough to overcome Him with human prowess, as he is the master (chief cause) who distributes pleasure and pain to all creatures.

devanagari: यो नो भवाय प्रागासीदभवाय दिवौकसाम् । स एव भगवानद्य वर्तते तद्विपर्ययम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yo no bhavāya prāgāsīdabhavāya divaukasāmsa eva bhagavānadya vartate tadviparyayam
translation: The glorious Lord who was formerly in favour of our prosperity and against that of the celestials, is today disposed to do quite the reverse of it.

devanagari: बलेन सचिवैर्बुद्ध्या दुर्गैर्मन्त्रौषधादिभिः । सामादिभिरुपायैश्च कालं नात्येति वै जनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: balena sacivairbuddhyā durgairmantrauṣadhādibhiḥsāmādibhirupāyaiśca kālaṃ nātyeti vai janaḥ
translation: No person can verily overcome the Time-spirit by physical power, expert advisers, intelligence, fortresses, mystic formulae, medicine etc. and by expedients like sāma, dāna, daṇḍa and bheda).

devanagari: भवद्भिर्निर्जिता ह्येते बहुशोऽनुचरा हरेः । दैवेनर्द्धैस्त एवाद्य युधि जित्वा नदन्ति नः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavadbhirnirjitā hyete bahuśo'nucarā hareḥdaivenarddhaista evādya yudhi jitvā nadanti naḥ
translation: These attendants of Hari have many times been vanquished by you, when Fortune was favourable to you. Today those very attendants are roaring after vanquishing you in the battle.

devanagari: एतान्वयं विजेष्यामो यदि दैवं प्रसीदति । तस्मात्कालं प्रतीक्षध्वं यो नोऽर्थत्वाय कल्पते ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: etānvayaṃ vijeṣyāmo yadi daivaṃ prasīdatitasmātkālaṃ pratīkṣadhvaṃ yo no'rthatvāya kalpate
translation: If the Providence becomes favourable (again), we shall again defeat them. Therefore wait for the favourable Time, which will turn to be advantageous to us.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । पत्युर्निगदितं श्रुत्वा दैत्यदानवयूथपाः । रसां निर्विविशू राजन्विष्णुपार्षद ताडिताः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācapatyurnigaditaṃ śrutvā daityadānavayūthapāḥrasāṃ nirviviśū rājanviṣṇupārṣada tāḍitāḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing the directive (speech) of their leader, the generals of the Daitya and Dānava forces, beaten by the attendants of Viṣṇu entered the Rasātala (nether world).

devanagari: अथ तार्क्ष्यसुतो ज्ञात्वा विराट्प्रभुचिकीर्षितम् । बबन्ध वारुणैः पाशैर्बलिं सूत्येऽहनि क्रतौ ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tārkṣyasuto jñātvā virāṭprabhucikīrṣitambabandha vāruṇaiḥ pāśairbaliṃ sūtye'hani kratau
translation: On the very day of extracting Soma juice in the sacrifice, Garuḍa, king of birds, the son of Tārkṣya, informed of the desire of the Lord[4], bound down Bali with the noosecords of Varuṇa.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: The Lord wanted to shower grace on Bali by depriving him of all his property and freeing him from his egoistic claim on his body. Or the Lord wanted to lower Bali’s position to exhibit Bali’s greatness etc.

devanagari: हाहाकारो महानासीद्रोदस्योः सर्वतो दिशम् । निगृह्यमाणेऽसुरपतौ विष्णुना प्रभविष्णुना ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: hāhākāro mahānāsīdrodasyoḥ sarvato diśamnigṛhyamāṇe'surapatau viṣṇunā prabhaviṣṇunā
translation: When the king of Asuras was thus being taken prisoner by Viṣṇu of superior power, there arose a loud outcry on earth, in the sky and in all directions.

devanagari: तं बद्धं वारुणैः पाशैर्भगवानाह वामनः । नष्टश्रियं स्थिरप्रज्ञमुदारयशसं नृप ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ baddhaṃ vāruṇaiḥ pāśairbhagavānāha vāmanaḥnaṣṭaśriyaṃ sthiraprajñamudārayaśasaṃ nṛpa
translation: Glorious Lord Vāmana addressed to Bali who, though bound down by the noose of Varuṇa and bereft of royal splendour, was of balanced and stable mind, and of noble reputation, Oh King!

devanagari: पदानि त्रीणि दत्तानि भूमेर्मह्यं त्वयासुर । द्वाभ्यां क्रान्ता मही सर्वा तृतीयमुपकल्पय ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: padāni trīṇi dattāni bhūmermahyaṃ tvayāsuradvābhyāṃ krāntā mahī sarvā tṛtīyamupakalpaya
translation: “Oh Asura! you have given me three paces of land. The entire earth (world) has been covered by two steps, Please provide space for the third.

devanagari: यावत्तपत्यसौ गोभिर्यावदिन्दुः सहोडुभिः । यावद्वर्षति पर्जन्यस्तावती भूरियं तव ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yāvattapatyasau gobhiryāvadinduḥ sahoḍubhiḥyāvadvarṣati parjanyastāvatī bhūriyaṃ tava
translation: As this entire land as far as the sun warms it by its rays, and the moon and the stars illuminate it, and as far as the rain-god was sending showers of water, was yours.

devanagari: पदैकेन मयाक्रान्तो भूर्लोकः खं दिशस्तनोः । स्वर्लोकस्ते द्वितीयेन पश्यतस्ते स्वमात्मना ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: padaikena mayākrānto bhūrlokaḥ khaṃ diśastanoḥsvarlokaste dvitīyena paśyataste svamātmanā
translation: With one step I have covered the region of the earth, the sky and the quarters by my body, the celestial regions were covered by the second step as witnessed by you.

devanagari: प्रतिश्रुतमदातुस्ते निरये वास इष्यते । विश त्वं निरयं तस्माद्गुरुणा चानुमोदितः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: pratiśrutamadātuste niraye vāsa iṣyateviśa tvaṃ nirayaṃ tasmādguruṇā cānumoditaḥ
translation: If you are not able to give what is promised, it is inevitable that your stay should be in the hell. Hence, you do enter the hell as approved of by your spiritual preceptor.

devanagari: वृथा मनोरथस्तस्य दूरः स्वर्गः पतत्यधः । प्रतिश्रुतस्यादानेन योऽर्थिनं विप्रलम्भते ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛthā manorathastasya dūraḥ svargaḥ patatyadhaḥpratiśrutasyādānena yo'rthinaṃ vipralambhate
translation: All the aspirations of that person fail. The celestial region is far away (from him). He falls down into hell who deceives a person seeking his help, by failing to give what is promised.

devanagari: विप्रलब्धो ददामीति त्वयाहं चाढ्यमानिना । तद्व्यलीकफलं भुङ्क्ष्व निरयं कतिचित्समाः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: vipralabdho dadāmīti tvayāhaṃ cāḍhyamāninātadvyalīkaphalaṃ bhuṅkṣva nirayaṃ katicitsamāḥ
translation:
Proud of your riches as you were, you deceived me with the words “I shall give you” (three paces of land). Suffer, therefore, for some years in hell as a punishment for uttering falsehood.”
